grades.expectation: Response notes the beneficiary's own extension as relevant to enforceability (acquiescence/waiver argument)
grades.reason: Clearly discusses acquiescence and waiver, and the 'clean hands' doctrine due to neighbor's 2015 extension

grades.expectation: Response discusses whether indemnity insurance is available and the 'no approach' prerequisite
grades.reason: Explicitly states the 'golden rule' that you must not contact the neighbor if seeking insurance, and discusses insurance as Option A

grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 LPA 1925 ground (aa) — covenant impedes reasonable use of land
grades.reason: Specifically mentions ground (aa) under Section 84 LPA 1925 about impeding reasonable user without substantial benefit

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes between a front/visible extension and rear extension in terms of risk
grades.reason: No distinction made between front/visible vs rear extensions - treats as general extension without considering visibility/location risk factors

grades.expectation: Response provides practical advice on sequencing: decide insurance vs release before any contact
grades.reason: Clear sequencing advice in final recommendation: review title, get insurance quote first, only contact neighbor if prepared to lose insurance option
